original_text: Allergies: Patient recorded as having No Known Allergies to Drugs Attending: ___. Chief Complaint: SMALL BOWEL OBSTRUCTION Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure: 1. Exploratory laparotomy. 2. Lysis of intraperitoneal adhesions History of Present Illness: This was a ___ woman, who in the remote past, had undergone a total abdominal hysterectomy for benign disease. ___ years prior to the present admission, she apparently had required hospitalization for a presumed small intestinal obstruction that had resolved without need for surgery. However, the patient presented to our emergency room 3 days prior to the present operation with a several-day history of abdominal discomfort, nausea and vomiting. A plain film of the abdomen followed by a CT scan suggested a distal fairly high-grade small bowel obstruction. She had essentially no gas in the colon and a convincing transition point in the very distal ileum with markedly dilated small intestine proximal to this. Her admission white blood cell count was elevated. She had mild right lower quadrant tenderness. I initially managed her with nasogastric decompression. However, her nasogastric tube was never very productive. She remained distended. On the third hospital day, her white blood count remained elevated at 14.6. She longer complained of discomfort. She actually had 1 or 2 small recorded bowel movements and reported passing flatus. However, she remained quite distended. A plain film the abdomen showed persistently dilated small intestine, measuring up to 5-6 cm. There was a paucity of gas within the colon. Although her situation was not completely straightforward, I found it disturbing that her white blood count remained elevated and that she had not made truly significant progress. Accordingly, I advised the patient and her family to undergo exploratory surgery to relieve her obstruction, and also to be certain that she did not have ischemic enteritis as a cause for her elevated white blood cell count. They freely consented to proceed. Past Medical History: Hypertension, Osteoporosis, Hyperlipidemia, Coronary artery disease, anxiety, Asthma, Small bowel obstruction treated conservatively ___ years ago.
